blocks:
Ayahs

قُلْ أَعُوذُ بِرَبِّ ٱلنَّاسِ (١)
Say, ‘I seek the protection of the Lord of humans, (1)
مَلِكِ ٱلنَّاسِ (٢)
Sovereign of humans, (2)
إِلَـٰهِ ٱلنَّاسِ (٣)
God of humans, (3)
مِن شَرِّ ٱلْوَسْوَاسِ ٱلْخَنَّاسِ (٤)
from the evil of the sneaky tempter (4)
ٱلَّذِى يُوَسْوِسُ فِى صُدُورِ ٱلنَّاسِ (٥)
who puts temptations into the breasts of humans, (5)
مِنَ ٱلْجِنَّةِ وَٱلنَّاسِ (٦)
from among the jinn and humans.’ (6)

Content and Virtue of Surah "An-Nas"

Man is always subject to satanic whisperings, and the devils among Jinn and men strive to penetrate his heart and soul. The higher a person's status in knowledge and the greater his position in society, the more intense the whisperings of the devils become, in order to deviate him from the path of truth and, through the corruption of a scholar, destroy an entire world.

This Surah commands the Noble Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, as an exemplar, a guide, and a leader, to seek refuge in Allah from the evil of all whisperers.

The content of this Surah is similar in one respect to Surah "Al-Falaq"; both concern seeking refuge in the Great Lord from evils and calamities, with the difference that in Surah Al-Falaq, various types of evils are mentioned, whereas in this Surah, the focus is solely on the evil of hidden whisperers (the retreating whisperer).

Regarding whether this Surah was revealed in Makkah or Madinah, there is again discussion among the commentators. A group considers it "Makki," and another group considers it "Madani," but the tone of its verses is more consistent with the Makki Surahs.

Furthermore, considering that this Surah and Surah Al-Falaq were revealed together according to narrations, and since Surah Al-Falaq is considered Makki by a large majority, this Surah could also be Makki.

Numerous narrations have been recorded regarding the virtue of reciting this Surah. Among them, we read in a Hadith that the Noble Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله became severely ill. Jibril and Mikail (two great angels of Allah) came to him; Jibril sat near the Prophet's head and Mikail near his feet. Jibril recited Surah "Al-Falaq" and placed the Prophet under Allah's protection with it, and Mikail recited Surah "قُلْ أَعُوذُ بِرَبِّ النَّاسِ" [1]. In a narration quoted from Imam al-Baqir عليه السلام, which we previously mentioned, we read:

"Whoever recites the 'Mu'awwidhatayn' (Surahs Al-Falaq and An-Nas) and 'قُلْ هُوَ اللَّهُ أَحَدٌ' in the Witr prayer, it is said to him: O servant of Allah! Glad tidings be to you, for Allah has accepted your Witr prayer!" [2]

[^1] and [^2]: "Nur al-Thaqalayn," Vol. 5, p. 7645, and "Majma' al-Bayan," Vol. 10, p. 569.

Commentary

I seek refuge in the Lord of mankind!

In this Surah, which is the final Surah of the Holy Quran, the address is directed to the person of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله as an exemplar, a model, and a leader of the people, saying:

"Say: I seek refuge in the Lord of mankind" (قُلْ أَعُوذُ بِرَبِّ النَّاسِ).

"In the Sovereign and Ruler of mankind" (مَلِكِ النَّاسِ).

"In the God and Deity of mankind" (إِلهِ النَّاسِ). It is noteworthy that here, emphasis is placed on three of the great Attributes of Allah (Lordship, Sovereignty, and Divinity), all of which have a direct connection to the upbringing of man and his salvation from the clutches of whisperers.

Of course, seeking refuge in Allah does not mean that a person merely says this phrase with the tongue; rather, he must also place himself under Allah's protection through thought, belief, and action. He must distance himself from satanic paths, satanic programs, satanic thoughts and propaganda, and satanic gatherings and circles, and place himself in the path of Divine thoughts and propaganda. Otherwise, a person who practically exposes himself to the storm of those whisperings will achieve nothing merely by reading this Surah and saying these words.

By saying "Lord of mankind," he confesses to the Lordship of the Sustainer and places himself under His upbringing.

By saying "Sovereign of mankind," he considers himself His property and becomes a servant obedient to His command.

And by saying "God of mankind," he steps onto the path of His servitude and avoids the worship of anything other than Him. Undoubtedly, whoever believes in these three attributes and harmonizes himself with all three will be safe from the evil of whisperers.

In reality, these three attributes are three important educational lessons, three preventive programs, and three means of salvation from the evil of whisperers, insuring man against them.

Therefore, in the next verse, He adds: "From the evil of the retreating whisperer" (مِنْ شَرِّ الْوَسْواسِ الْخَنَّاسِ).

"The one who whispers into the breasts of mankind" (الَّذِي يُوَسْوِسُ فِي صُدُورِ النَّاسِ).

"Whisperers from among the Jinn or mankind" (مِنَ الْجِنَّةِ وَ النَّاسِ). The word "Waswas," according to Raghib in "Al-Mufradat," originally refers to the faint sound produced by the clinking of ornaments! Later, it was applied to any faint sound, and after that, it was used for the evil and undesirable thoughts and notions that arise in the heart and soul of man, resembling a faint sound whispered into the ear.

"Waswas" has an infinitive meaning, but sometimes it is also used as a "subject" (whisperer), and in the verse under discussion, it carries this meaning.

"Khannas" is an intensive form from the root "Khunus" (on the pattern of Khusuf), meaning to shrink back and retreat. This is because the devils retreat when the name of Allah is mentioned, and since this act is often accompanied by hiding, this word has also come to mean "concealment."

Therefore, the concept of the verses is: "Say: I seek refuge in Allah from the evil of the devilish whisperer who flees and hides from the name of Allah."

Fundamentally, "devils" mix their programs with secrecy, and sometimes they breathe into the ear of man's soul in such a way that man believes the thought is his own, springing from within his soul, and this very thing leads to his seduction and misguidance! The work of the devil is beautification (tazyin) and hiding falsehood in a coating of truth, lies in a shell of honesty, sin in the garment of worship, and misguidance in the cover of guidance.

In short, they themselves are hidden, and their programs are also concealed. This is a warning to all wayfarers of the path of truth not to expect to see the devils in their original face and appearance, or to observe their programs in a deviant form. It is never so; they are the "Waswas al-Khannas," and their work is trickery, lies, deception, hypocrisy, pretension, and hiding the truth.

If they were to appear in their "original face," if they did not mix "falsehood" with "truth," and if they spoke "clearly and plainly," then as Ali عليه السلام said: "The matter would not be hidden from those seeking the path of Allah!" They always take a portion of "this" and a portion of "that" and mix them together to gain mastery over the people, as the Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام says in the continuation of this speech: "It is there that Satan gains mastery over his allies." [1]

[^1]: "Nahj al-Balagha," Sermon 50.

The expression "الَّذِي يُوَسْوِسُ فِي صُدُورِ النَّاسِ" and the choice of the word "Waswasah" and the word "Sudur" (breasts) is also an emphasis on this meaning.

All of this is on one hand; on the other hand, the phrase "مِنَ الْجِنَّةِ وَ النَّاسِ" warns that the "retreating whisperers" are not only among one group or community, or in one class or garment. They are scattered among Jinn and men and are found in every garment and every community. One must be wary of all of them and seek refuge in Allah from the evil of all of them.

Bad friends, deviant companions, misguided and tyrannical leaders, agents of oppressors and tyrants, corrupt writers and speakers, deceptive atheistic and syncretic schools of thought, whispering mass media—all of these and others are included in the broad concept of "Waswas al-Khannas" from whose evil man must seek refuge in Allah.

Key Points

1- Why do we seek refuge in Allah?!

At every moment, the possibility of deviation exists for man, and fundamentally, when Allah commands His Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله to seek refuge in Allah from the evil of the "Waswas al-Khannas," it is proof of the possibility of falling into the trap of the retreating ones and whisperers.

Even though the Noble Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله was insured against any kind of deviation by Divine grace and unseen aid, and by entrusting himself to Allah, he nevertheless recited these verses and sought refuge in Him from the evil of the retreating whisperers. Given this, the duty of others is clear.

However, one must not despair, for against these destructive whisperers, the angels of heaven come to the aid of the believing servants and the wayfarers of the path of truth. Yes, the believers are not alone; angels descend upon them and help them: "Indeed, those who have said, 'Our Lord is Allah' and then remained on a right course—the angels will descend upon them" (Fussilat: 30).

But in any case, one must never become arrogant or consider himself independent of exhortation, advice, remembrance, and Divine aid. One must always seek refuge in Him, always be awake, and always be vigilant.

2- Regarding why "Nas" (mankind) is repeated in three verses, some have said it is because in each case it carries a different meaning.

However, it appears that it is for emphasis on the universality of these three attributes of Allah, and in all three cases, it has a single meaning.

3- In a narration from the Noble Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, we read: "There is no believer except that his heart in his breast has two ears: an ear into which the angel breathes, and an ear into which the retreating whisperer breathes. Allah supports the believer through the angel, and that is His saying: 'And He supported them with a spirit from Him.'" [1] In a meaningful and stirring Hadith from Imam al-Sadiq عليه السلام, we read: "When the verse: 'And those who, when they commit an immorality or wrong themselves, remember Allah and seek forgiveness for their sins' was revealed, Iblis went atop a mountain in Makkah and cried out with a loud voice, gathering the leaders of his army.

They said: O our master! What has happened that you have called us?

He said: This verse has been revealed (a verse that makes my back tremble and is a means of salvation for mankind). Who can confront it?

One of the great devils said: I can, my plan is such and such! Iblis did not approve of his plan! Another stood up and presented his plan; it too was not accepted! At this point, the 'Waswas al-Khannas' stood up and said: I can handle it.

Iblis said: By what means?

He said: I will keep them busy with promises and desires until they become tainted with sin, and when they commit sin, I will make them forget repentance! Iblis said: You are capable of this task (your plan is very skillful and excellent), and he entrusted this mission to him until the Day of Resurrection." [1]

O Allah! Protect us from the evil of all these whisperers and from the evil of all "retreating whisperers." O Lord! The trap is difficult, the enemy is awake, and his plans are hidden and secret, and salvation is not possible except by Your grace.

O God! We do not know how to offer thanks for this great blessing at Your threshold, that You bestowed favor upon us and granted us this great honor and success to complete this Tafsir at this hour and after about 15 years.

O Allah! You know that at this moment, an indescribable vitality, a joy and delight mixed with gratitude, surges through our entire being—a feeling that we have no power to explain or thank with any expression. We raise our hands to Your threshold and say: O Creator! It is possible that we have fallen into errors in the interpretation of these verses; forgive us for all of them, and we hope Your servants will also forgive us.

And in the final sentence, we say: O Merciful and Compassionate God, accept this humble service from all of us by Your generosity, and make it a treasure for our return and the Day of Recompense. And our final prayer is: Praise be to Allah, Lord of the Worlds. End of Surah An-Nas and end of Volume 27.

On the date of 1366/04/14 (July 5, 1987), corresponding to the eighth of Dhu al-Qi'dah 1407 AH, Tafsir Nemouneh was completed.
